ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status
Off Calendar Feb-25-2008 Continued To Mar-24-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joel Blackman for the San Francisco Public Guardian (SFPG). Nancy Fields for the Conservatee.

The Court stated that the conservatorship needed to be terminated, and that the SFPG'S first and final account was needed, and that the first and final account on both trusts were also needed. Mr. Blackman stated that he was not sure if any property from the old trust was transferred to the new trust, that he was ready to do a schedule for the first and final on the old trust, and that he would file a new petition to terminate by March 24, 2008. Ms. Fields stated that her client was very upset, that things were not moving fast enough, and that she would like to see the petition to terminate by March 10, 2008. The Court will allow counsel to determine who will file the petition to terminate. The Court approved the petition appointing Anne F. Smith the successor trustee. A hearing date of March 24, 2008 is set for status on the petition to terminate. and the accountings for all 3 three matters.

Judge Pro Tem: Sue Kaplan. Reporter: Maria Letora CSR 11935. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Hill.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status
Off Calendar Jan-14-2008 Continued To Feb-25-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status
Off Calendar Dec-03-2007 Continued To Jan-14-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case #287624 (Conservatorship of Lillian Kearney).

Appearances: Joel Blackman for the San Francisco Public Guardian ("SFPG"). Nancy Field for the Conservatee.

Ms. Field reported that she received a copy of the executed trust, that the petition was prepared, that the successor trustee had declined serving and that a professional fiduciary had been chosen. Mr. Blackman reported that the house was being paid for by the SFPG. The Court directed Mr. Blackman to file a first and final account on the old trust and to transfer the assets to the new trust which should have a new case number. An inventory needs to be done on the new trust as well. Ms. Field will file the petition amending the trust on the new case number. In addition, Mr. Blackman must file an accounting for the Conservatorship matter, and transfer the assets to the new trust. Both matters were continued to January 14, 2007.

Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Rich Lee.
